title: ranunculus caucasicus subsp. caucasicus
intro: Ranunculus caucasicus subsp. caucasicus, also known as Caucasian buttercup, is a perennial plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and shiny leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus caucasicus subsp. caucasicus include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in water or oil to create a topical cream or ointment, which can be applied directly to affected areas.
